args.body:
Dear Alice,

I hope this message finds you well. As part of your ongoing care, I wanted to share some important information regarding shared family health risks.

--- Shared-Family Guidance Template ---

Family Risk Overview:
Certain health conditions can have a hereditary or shared environmental component. Based on your clinical history and family background, the following guidance applies:

1. Genetic & Hereditary Risks: Some conditions may be passed down through family lines. It is important that close relatives are aware of any relevant diagnoses so they can discuss screening options with their own healthcare providers.

2. Shared Environmental Factors: Family members living in the same household may share exposure to environmental risk factors (e.g., diet, lifestyle, environmental exposures). Addressing these together as a family can improve outcomes for everyone.

3. Recommended Actions:
   - Inform close family members of any relevant hereditary conditions identified during your care.
   - Encourage family members to schedule their own preventive screenings.
   - Maintain open communication with your care team about any new family health developments.

4. Confidentiality Notice: Please be mindful that sharing your own health information with family members is your personal decision. Your provider will not share your information without your explicit consent.

5. Next Steps: Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have questions or would like to discuss this further with your care team.

Warm regards,
Your Care Team

Dear Alice,

I hope this message finds you well. As part of your ongoing care, I wanted to share some important information regarding shared family health risks.

--- Shared-Family Guidance Template ---

Family Risk Overview:
Certain health conditions can have a hereditary or shared environmental component. Based on your clinical history and family background, the following guidance applies:

1. Genetic & Hereditary Risks: Some conditions may be passed down through family lines. It is important that close relatives are aware of any relevant diagnoses so they can discuss screening options with their own healthcare providers.

2. Shared Environmental Factors: Family members living in the same household may share exposure to environmental risk factors (e.g., diet, lifestyle, environmental exposures). Addressing these together as a family can improve outcomes for everyone.

3. Recommended Actions:
   - Inform close family members of any relevant hereditary conditions identified during your care.
   - Encourage family members to schedule their own preventive screenings.
   - Maintain open communication with your care team about any new family health developments.

4. Confidentiality Notice: Please be mindful that sharing your own health information with family members is your personal decision. Your provider will not share your information without your explicit consent.

5. Next Steps: Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have questions or would like to discuss this further with your care team.

Warm regards,
Your Care Team
